title: National policies for orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration
subtitle: The United Nations measures whether national migration policies meet International Organization for Migration criteria in a variety of policy domains, to ensure orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration.
caption:
National policies for orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration

The United Nations measures whether national migration policies meet International Organization for Migration criteria in a variety of policy domains, to ensure orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration.

This choropleth world map displays the status of national migration policies in 2021, categorized by how well they meet International Organization for Migration criteria. The legend defines five distinct categories: "No data" (indicated by diagonal hatching), "Requires further progress" (dark red), "Partially meets criteria" (orange), "Meets criteria" (light blue), and "Fully meets criteria" (dark blue). A significant portion of the map, including the United States, Canada, Greenland, Russia, and large areas of Africa and the Middle East, is covered in diagonal hatching, indicating no available data for these regions. Among the countries with data, the light blue "Meets criteria" band is the most widespread, covering Australia, most of Europe, and the majority of South America. The dark blue "Fully meets criteria" category is assigned to a smaller set of nations, including Peru, South Africa, Iran, and Myanmar. The orange "Partially meets criteria" band appears in scattered locations such as Mexico, Brazil, India, and China. The darkest red band, representing "Requires further progress," is visible in a few specific countries, including Yemen, Sudan, and Libya. The visual distribution shows that while many nations meet the basic criteria, very few fully meet them, and a substantial number of countries lack reported data entirely.
